artifact_scope: source-model proof that transformed bytes do not imply authority, source retirement, degraded-read safety, or successor wording before receipts say so

boundaries.proof: Storage-intent policy authority defines receipts as earned evidence, not labels. A transform frame, digest name, ciphertext, shard, coalesced record, or rebaked layout is only an implementation artifact until a result record names policy, evidence cut, decision, receipt or blocker, response projection, retry law, and retention proof.
boundaries.required_to_strengthen: current manifest-backed runtime evidence and a pass claim status for the exact scope

boundaries.proof: Background layout, cost, wear, availability, coalescing, or rebake work may improve physical shape only after hard legality gates pass and source-retirement rules preserve old authority until replacement authority is published.
boundaries.required_to_strengthen: cutover receipt, old/new byte proof, rollback/refusal record, and source-retirement proof

boundaries.proof: A caller-visible reply must project an earned receipt, degraded-visible state, or typed refusal. EC/archive shards, cache hits, or transformed media cannot become degraded-read safety without result/refusal evidence naming the degraded state and policy permission.
boundaries.required_to_strengthen: degraded reconstruction or recall runtime rows plus result/refusal projection

boundaries.proof: The issue comment for #1981 keeps OpenZFS/Ceph successor and comparator consumption out of this slice. Any future data-shape consumption by successor wording belongs through #2048 after #2046 and #2047 validate or explicitly remain blocked.
boundaries.required_to_strengthen: separate successor/comparator evidence and claims-gate updates outside this issue

forbidden_inferences: matching digest implies legal dedupe authority
EC/archive label implies availability or low-latency serving
data-shape evidence implies OpenZFS/Ceph successor wording
claim_effect: This source-model boundary review passes only as a fail-closed result/refusal model; it does not validate runtime transform execution or performance/fault rows.
